# Zero-dep allowlist (roadmap item 31 — per-crate extension of items 1+13).
# INVARIANT: this file may only SHRINK. CI (scripts/zero-dep-gate.sh <crate>) compares HEAD
# against origin/main and fails if any name here is absent there (= growth). The ONLY
# sanctioned growth path is editing the `zero-dep-gate` job in .github/workflows/ci.yml in
# the same reviewed diff. Names-only + full transitive closure of the DEFAULT no-dev build
# (version bumps do not churn this file; a NEW transitive crate via `cargo update` goes RED).
# Generated by: cargo tree -e no-dev --locked --offline --prefix none | grep -v " (/" | awk "{print \$1}" | sort -u
# Ruling records use the slot_arena.rs comment format (item-25 step 9).
#
# RULING (item 31 §4.4 — CORRECTED 2026-07-19, evidence over the blueprint's projection): serde
#   carrier. The blueprint listed wasm as a Phase-A cutover target. Independent verification of the
#   real code REJECTS Phase-A for wasm: the SpineDoc site (lib.rs:143) is trivially cuttable, BUT the
#   `a11y_build_mirror` site (lib.rs:490) DEserializes `dowiz_engine::semantics::SemanticScene` AND
#   SERIALIZES the mirror `A11yTree` back — both through the SHARED `dowiz-engine` crate's `serde`
#   feature. A kernel::json cutover there would require hand-rolling (de)serialization of engine-owned
#   types, coupling kernel::json to engine's schema (a WORSE design) — or duplicating engine's serde
#   derives. A partial cutover (SpineDoc only) removes NOTHING from the tree (serde stays for
#   SemanticScene). So wasm is DEFERRED to Phase-B. Reopening trigger: when `dowiz-engine` exposes a
#   serde-free (de)serialization codec for SemanticScene/A11yTree (or that site moves to a
#   kernel-owned type), cut wasm over then. wasm-bindgen pinned =0.2.95 to the offline CLI.
bumpalo
cfg-if
itoa
log
memchr
once_cell
proc-macro2
quote
serde
serde_core
serde_derive
serde_json
syn
unicode-ident
wasm-bindgen
wasm-bindgen-backend
wasm-bindgen-macro
wasm-bindgen-macro-support
wasm-bindgen-shared
zmij
